content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You may choose an arbitrary permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã of the integers 111 through NNN. Then, for each vertex iii, you should assign the value ApiA_{p_i}Api‚Äã‚Äã to this vertex.

The profit of a path between two vertices uuu and vvv is the sum of the values assigned to the vertices on that path (including uuu and vvv).

Let's consider only (undirected) paths that start at a leaf and end at a different leaf. Calculate the maximum possible value of the sum of profits of all such paths. Since this value could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
Each of the following N‚àí1N‚àí1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum sum of profits, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: (1,4,2,3)(1, 4, 2, 3)(1,4,2,3) is one of the possible permutations that give the optimal answer. Then, the profits of paths between pairs of vertices (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) are 777, 888 and 999 respectively.
Example case 2: Here, any permutation could be chosen.
